subject: PhD Algebra
instructions:
Do seven of the following eleven problems. Do not turn in more than seven problems. You must show your work. Answers with no work and/or no explanations will receive no credit. State clearly any theorem you use in your proofs.

In the problems, \(\mathbb Z\), resp. \(\mathbb Q\), \(\mathbb C\), is the set of all integers, resp. of all rational numbers, of all complex numbers.

problems.text: State and prove the Jordan–Hölder theorem for finite groups.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that the additive group of a field is never a free abelian group.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that the multiplicative group of positive rational numbers is a free abelian group, and find a basis.

problems.text: A category \(C\) is small if its class of objects is a set, and skeletal if for all \(X\) and \(Y\) in \(C\), \(X\simeq Y\) implies \(X=Y\). Show that any small category is equivalent to a skeletal one (choose an object from each isomorphism class).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what it means for an \(R\)-module \(M\) to be flat.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that the polynomial ring \(R[x]\), considered as an \(R\)-module in the natural way, is flat.

problems.text: Show that if \(R\) is a commutative ring with identity and every prime ideal of \(R\) is finitely generated, then \(R\) is Noetherian.

Show that for any extension \(A\to B\) of commutative rings, any \(A\)-module \(M\) and any \(B\)-module \(N\), there is an isomorphism
\[
\operatorname{Hom}_B(M\otimes_A B,N)\simeq \operatorname{Hom}_A(M,N_A)
\]
functorial in \(M\) and \(N\) (here \(N_A\) denotes \(N\) considered as an \(A\)-module by means of the ring homomorphism \(A\to B\)).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that if \(L/F\), \(K/L\) are finite separable extensions, then so is \(K/F\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Do the same for purely inseparable extensions.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that if \(K/F\) is a normal extension and \(F\subseteq L\subseteq K\) is an intermediate field, then \(K/L\) is normal.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example where \(L/F\) is not normal.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Give an example of normal extensions \(L/F\), \(K/L\) where \(K/F\) is not normal.

problems.text: Let \(p\) be a prime and \(K\) be the splitting field over \(\mathbb Q\) of \(X^p-1\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that \(K\) contains a unique quadratic extension of \(\mathbb Q\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: When is this quadratic extension real? (You may assume as known the structure of the Galois group of \(K/\mathbb Q\). hint: if \(\zeta\) is a primitive \(p\)th root of unity, complex conjugation sends \(\zeta\mapsto\zeta^{-1}\)).

problems.text: Let \(k\) be a field and \(R\) a finite-dimensional \(k\)-algebra. Show that if \(R\) is semisimple as a ring, it is a direct sum of extension fields of \(k\).
Classify up to isomorphism all semisimple rings with \(720\) elements.
